meaning: n.副业,边界线,兼职,附带销售的货物,局外人的观点adj.倾斜的vt.迫使退出
examples: It was quite a lucrative sideline.
I imagined Emily laughing from the sideline, and suddenly I really did feel sick to my stomach.
Since his regular job was boring, I asked him why he just didn't do his sideline full-time.
And you seemed concerned that this might further sideline any peace efforts in that region.
X-ray and ultrasound machines, helped by earnings from Mr Zhou's sideline business growing flowers and trees.
